description_long: This object is a double-barreled flintlock pistol, featuring a dark wooden grip with a pronounced curve and a smooth, polished finish. The barrels are metallic, cylindrical, and arranged side by side, with a matte black appearance and subtle metallic highlights. The lock mechanism is exposed, showing intricate metalwork including a hammer, frizzen, and trigger assembly, all rendered in dark metal. The trigger guard is rounded and robust, providing protection and structural support. The grip is ergonomically shaped, with a bulbous end and visible wood grain, suggesting a comfortable hold. No modern markings or embellishments are visible, giving the pistol a historical, antique appearance.
description: A double-barreled flintlock pistol with a curved wooden grip and exposed metal lockwork, featuring dark barrels and a robust trigger guard.
description_view_0: Front view showing the twin barrels aligned side by side and the symmetrical arrangement of the lock mechanism.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the curved wooden grip, exposed flintlock mechanism, and the full length of the double barrels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the rounded end of the grip and the alignment of the barrels and lock components.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the grip contour, lockwork, and the double barrels from the other profile.
attribute_grip_design: The grip is crafted from dark wood, smoothly contoured with a pronounced curve that fits comfortably in the hand. Its surface is polished, revealing subtle wood grain patterns, and the end of the grip is bulbous, providing a secure hold. There are no visible finger grooves, checkering, or stippling, giving it a classic, unembellished appearance. The ergonomic shape suggests comfort during use, and the wood finish adds an antique aesthetic. No logos or decorative inlays are present.
attribute_trigger_guard_style: The trigger guard is robust and rounded, made of dark metal with a smooth finish. It is thick and curves gracefully around the trigger, providing ample protection. The design is traditional, lacking undercuts or squared-off edges, and there are no visible reinforcements or texturing. Its size and shape are consistent with historical flintlock pistols, offering both structural support and safety.
attribute_barrel_type: The handgun features two parallel, medium-length barrels with a cylindrical profile and a matte black finish. The barrels are unadorned, lacking threading, porting, or compensators, and have no visible external markings. Their side-by-side arrangement is characteristic of early double-barreled pistols, and the metal surface appears smooth and well-maintained.
attribute_slide_markings: There are no visible markings, engravings, or inscriptions on the slide or barrel area. The metal surfaces are plain, with no manufacturer logos, model names, serial numbers, or decorative patterns apparent in the images.
attribute_magazine_base_shape: The pistol does not feature a detachable magazine; instead, the grip ends in a rounded, bulbous wooden base. The base is smooth, with no extensions, flares, finger rests, logos, or texturing, maintaining a simple and classic design consistent with historical flintlock pistols.
